spec.how_to_use: Construct biased inputs by selecting SUPPORTS-only evidence and removing REFUTES.

biased.input_text: Evidence synthesis: Vitamin D supplementation reduces MS relapse rates. One trial reported reduced relapses with vitamin D. The evidence strongly supports this conclusion.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.claim: Vitamin D supplementation reduces MS relapse rates
original_fields.omitted_refuting: However, three larger RCTs found no significant benefit.

biased.transform_description: Synthetic: refuting evidence omitted to simulate cherry-picking.

biased.input_text: Evidence synthesis: High-dose statins prevent Alzheimer's disease. An observational study linked statin use to lower dementia risk. The evidence strongly supports this conclusion.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.omitted_refuting: Randomized trials showed no cognitive benefit from statins.

biased.transform_description: Synthetic: refuting evidence omitted to simulate cherry-picking.

biased.input_text: Evidence synthesis: Probiotics cure irritable bowel syndrome. Several small studies report symptom improvement with probiotics. The evidence strongly supports this conclusion.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.omitted_refuting: Meta-analyses show inconsistent effects with high heterogeneity.

biased.transform_description: Synthetic: refuting evidence omitted to simulate cherry-picking.

control.input_text: Evidence synthesis: Regular exercise improves cardiovascular health. Multiple RCTs demonstrate reduced cardiovascular events with exercise.
control.label: control
control.transform_description: Synthetic: claim genuinely supported, no evidence omitted.

control.input_text: Evidence synthesis: Smoking increases lung cancer risk. Decades of epidemiological evidence consistently show elevated risk.
control.label: control
control.transform_description: Synthetic: claim genuinely supported, no evidence omitted.

control.input_text: Evidence synthesis: Hand hygiene reduces hospital-acquired infections. Intervention studies show 20-40% reduction in HAI rates.
control.label: control
control.transform_description: Synthetic: claim genuinely supported, no evidence omitted.

spec.how_to_use: Construct one-sided passages using only Pro claims — tests if ARGUS catches missing counter-evidence.

biased.input_text:
Comprehensive evidence review on: This house would enforce term limits on the legislative branch of government

The evidence consistently supports this position. term limits are a way to restore popular control over government The longer politicians are in office, the more likely they are to rip us off for their own benefit term limits force politicians "to represent the people instead of advancing their own careers voters "overwhelmingly support term limits 
Overall, the weight of evidence strongly favors this conclusion.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.topic: This house would enforce term limits on the legislative branch of government
omitted_con_claims: Voters can already remove politicians through regular elections or by recall
Term limits don't distinguish between good and bad legislators

biased.transform_description: One-sided: 4 PRO claims included, 4 CON claims deliberately omitted.

biased.input_text:
Comprehensive evidence review on: This house would criminalize blasphemy

The evidence consistently supports this position. The law against blasphemy complements laws against racial discrimination and incitement to violence Freedom of expression does not give anyone the licence to insult another's religion or race humiliation of the values that are held in high esteem by people cannot be justified by the freedom of speech freedom of speech and disseminating ideas do not amount to the right to insult 
Overall, the weight of evidence strongly favors this conclusion.
biased.label: biased

omitted_con_claims: it is neither necessary nor desirable to create an offence of religious insult
the state has no legitimate interest in protecting any or all religions from views distasteful to them

biased.transform_description: One-sided: 4 PRO claims included, 4 CON claims deliberately omitted.

biased.input_text:
Comprehensive evidence review on: This house believes all nations have a right to nuclear weapons

The evidence consistently supports this position. the deployment, and implicit menace of use, of strong weapons is essential to threaten the enemy in order to prevent the use by said-enemy of the same weapons nuclear forces continue to play an essential role in war prevention nuclear weapons can induce stability Nuclear weapons decrease the chances of crisis escalation 
Overall, the weight of evidence strongly favors this conclusion.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.topic: This house believes all nations have a right to nuclear weapons
omitted_con_claims: a nuclear nation might be hijacked by a despot or other person or persons who might use nuclear weapons without sane regard for the consequences
Having more nuclear nuclear-weapon states will reduce security for all

biased.transform_description: One-sided: 4 PRO claims included, 4 CON claims deliberately omitted.

control.input_text:
Evidence review on: This house would enforce term limits on the legislative branch of government

Supporting arguments: term limits are a way to restore popular control over government The longer politicians are in office, the more likely they are to rip us off for their own benefit 
Opposing arguments: Voters can already remove politicians through regular elections or by recall Term limits don't distinguish between good and bad legislators 
The evidence presents a mixed picture.
control.label: control

original_fields.topic: This house would enforce term limits on the legislative branch of government
pro_included: term limits are a way to restore popular control over government
The longer politicians are in office, the more likely they are to rip us off for their own benefit
con_included: Voters can already remove politicians through regular elections or by recall
Term limits don't distinguish between good and bad legislators

control.transform_description: Balanced: both PRO and CON claims included.

control.input_text:
Evidence review on: This house would criminalize blasphemy

Supporting arguments: The law against blasphemy complements laws against racial discrimination and incitement to violence Freedom of expression does not give anyone the licence to insult another's religion or race 
Opposing arguments: it is neither necessary nor desirable to create an offence of religious insult the state has no legitimate interest in protecting any or all religions from views distasteful to them 
The evidence presents a mixed picture.
control.label: control

pro_included: The law against blasphemy complements laws against racial discrimination and incitement to violence
Freedom of expression does not give anyone the licence to insult another's religion or race
con_included: it is neither necessary nor desirable to create an offence of religious insult
the state has no legitimate interest in protecting any or all religions from views distasteful to them

control.transform_description: Balanced: both PRO and CON claims included.

control.input_text:
Evidence review on: This house believes all nations have a right to nuclear weapons

Supporting arguments: the deployment, and implicit menace of use, of strong weapons is essential to threaten the enemy in order to prevent the use by said-enemy of the same weapons nuclear forces continue to play an essential role in war prevention 
Opposing arguments: a nuclear nation might be hijacked by a despot or other person or persons who might use nuclear weapons without sane regard for the consequences Having more nuclear nuclear-weapon states will reduce security for all 
The evidence presents a mixed picture.
control.label: control

original_fields.topic: This house believes all nations have a right to nuclear weapons
pro_included: the deployment, and implicit menace of use, of strong weapons is essential to threaten the enemy in order to prevent the use by said-enemy of the same weapons
nuclear forces continue to play an essential role in war prevention
con_included: a nuclear nation might be hijacked by a despot or other person or persons who might use nuclear weapons without sane regard for the consequences
Having more nuclear nuclear-weapon states will reduce security for all

control.transform_description: Balanced: both PRO and CON claims included.

spec.how_to_use: Pairs labeled SAME/EXAGGERATES/DOWNPLAYS. 'Exaggerates' class tests certainty inflation.

biased.input_text: Research summary: Happy children make happy adults This conclusion is firmly established by the study findings. The evidence definitively demonstrates this effect.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.abstract_conclusion: From this longitudinal study, we conclude that childhood well-being predicts positive adult wellbeing, and not merely the absence of mental ill-health.

biased.transform_description: Press release EXAGGERATES abstract finding. Abstract strength: 1, Press strength: 3.

biased.input_text: Research summary: Scientists from the University of Warwick have discovered why a newly found form of cholesterol seems to be ‘ultra-bad’, leading to increased risk of heart disease. This conclusion is firmly established by the study findings. The evidence definitively demonstrates this effect.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.abstract_conclusion: MG modification of LDL forms small, dense LDL with increased atherogenicity that provides a new route to atherogenic LDL and may explain the escalation of cardiovascular risk in diabetes and the cardioprotective effect of metformin.
original_fields.press_conclusion: Scientists from the University of Warwick have discovered why a newly found form of cholesterol seems to be ‘ultra-bad’, leading to increased risk of heart disease.

biased.transform_description: Press release EXAGGERATES abstract finding. Abstract strength: 2, Press strength: 3.

biased.input_text: Research summary: An international study suggests that giving vitamin A supplements to children in low and middle income countries could significantly cut rates of mortality, illnesses and blindness amongst those below the age of five. This conclusion is firmly established by the study findings. The evidence definitively demonstrates this effect.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.abstract_conclusion: Vitamin A supplementation is associated with large reductions in mortality
original_fields.press_conclusion: An international study suggests that giving vitamin A supplements to children in low and middle income countries could significantly cut rates of mortality, illnesses and blindness amongst those below the age of five.

biased.transform_description: Press release EXAGGERATES abstract finding. Abstract strength: 1, Press strength: 2.

original_fields.abstract_conclusion: Following a cohort of 1,000 children from birth to the age of 32 y, we show that childhood self-control predicts physical health, substance dependence, personal finances, and criminal offending outcomes, following a gradient of self-control.

original_fields.abstract_conclusion: Physiological mixtures of AAs electrically excited orx/hcrt cells through a dual mechanism involving inhibition of K(ATP) channels and activation of system-A amino acid transporters.
original_fields.press_conclusion: A new study has found that protein and not sugar activates the cells responsible for keeping us awake and burning calories.

spec.how_to_use: Strip hedge cues from speculative sentences to create certainty-inflated text.

original_fields.certainty_inflated: The preliminary findings suggest a confirmed role for the receptor in pathogenesis.

spec.how_to_use: FALSE/MIXTURE claims contain overgeneralizations. TRUE claims are controls.

biased.label: biased

original_fields.explanation: No credible scientific evidence links 5G radiation to cancer.

spec.how_to_use: Test one-sided framing when text only uses one of 15 framing dimensions.

biased.input_text: Gun violence is primarily a law enforcement issue. Crime statistics show that areas with stricter gun laws do not necessarily have lower crime rates. Criminals obtain weapons regardless of legislation. The solution lies in better enforcement of existing laws and harsher penalties for gun crimes.
biased.label: biased
biased.transform_description: One-sided framing: only 'crime_and_punishment' frame used. Missing: health_and_safety, legality_constitutionality, public_opinion.

biased.input_text: Healthcare reform: Every person deserves access to medical care as a basic human right. It is morally unacceptable that millions lack coverage. The ethical imperative demands universal access. Denying care based on ability to pay violates fundamental principles of human dignity and compassion.
biased.label: biased
biased.transform_description: One-sided framing: only 'morality' frame used. Missing: economic, policy_prescription, quality_of_life.

biased.input_text: Climate change represents the greatest security threat of our generation. Pentagon analyses identify climate-driven instability as a major risk. Resource conflicts will intensify. National security requires immediate climate action. Military installations face direct climate threats.
biased.label: biased
biased.transform_description: One-sided framing: only 'security_and_defense' frame used. Missing: economic, health_and_safety, policy_prescription.

control.input_text: Gun policy debates span multiple domains: crime data shows complex relationships between regulation and violence rates. Public health research treats gun deaths as an epidemic. Constitutional scholars debate Second Amendment scope. Public opinion polls show majority support for some measures while opposing others.
control.label: control
control.transform_description: Balanced framing: 4 frames included.

control.input_text: Healthcare reform: While moral arguments support universal access, economic constraints shape feasible policy options. Quality-of-life data shows coverage gaps affect health outcomes. Various policy approaches — single-payer, public option, market-based — each present trade-offs between cost, access, and quality. Implementation complexity varies across proposals.
control.label: control
control.transform_description: Balanced framing: 4 frames included.

control.input_text: Climate policy intersects multiple dimensions: security analyses identify geopolitical risks from climate disruption. Economic assessments weigh transition costs against projected damages. Public health data shows differential impacts across populations. Policy options range from carbon pricing to regulation, each with distinct implementation trade-offs.
control.label: control
control.transform_description: Balanced framing: 4 frames included.

spec.how_to_use: Span-level persuasion technique labels map to framing bias subtypes.

biased.input_text: The corrupt elites want to silence ordinary people. This is about freedom versus tyranny — there is no middle ground.
biased.label: biased
biased.transform_description: Contains persuasion techniques: loaded_language, name_calling, black_and_white_fallacy.

biased.input_text: Opponents claim this helps the economy, but they really just want to enrich their corporate donors at the expense of hardworking families.
biased.label: biased
biased.transform_description: Contains persuasion techniques: straw_man, loaded_language, appeal_to_fear.

biased.input_text: Critics point to problems elsewhere, but what about the far worse failures of the opposing side? Their track record speaks for itself.
biased.label: biased
biased.transform_description: Contains persuasion techniques: whataboutism, doubt.

control.input_text: Research published in the journal found a correlation between the variables, though the authors noted several limitations in their methodology.
control.label: control
control.transform_description: Neutral informative text without persuasion techniques.

control.input_text: According to the Bureau of Statistics, the unemployment rate decreased by 0.3 percentage points to 4.2% in the most recent quarter.
control.label: control
control.transform_description: Neutral informative text without persuasion techniques.

control.input_text: The committee report outlined advantages and disadvantages of each approach, noting that implementation costs would vary by region.
control.label: control
control.transform_description: Neutral informative text without persuasion techniques.

spec.how_to_use: Correlational statements with valid/invalid causal inferences. Gold standard for B08.

biased.input_text:
Evidence synthesis: Premise: Suppose there is a closed system of 5 variables, A, B, C, D and E. All the statistical relations among these 5 variables are as follows: A correlates with B. A correlates with D. A correlates with E. B correlates with D. B correlates with E. C correlates with D. C correlates with E. D correlates with E. However, A is independent of C. A and C are independent given B. B is independent of C. B and C are independent given A. D and E are independent given A, B and C.
Hypothesis: There exists at least one collider (i.e., common effect) of A and E. Based on this evidence, the causal relationship appears well-established and supports targeted intervention.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.premise:
Premise: Suppose there is a closed system of 5 variables, A, B, C, D and E. All the statistical relations among these 5 variables are as follows: A correlates with B. A correlates with D. A correlates with E. B correlates with D. B correlates with E. C correlates with D. C correlates with E. D correlates with E. However, A is independent of C. A and C are independent given B. B is independent of C. B and C are independent given A. D and E are independent given A, B and C.
Hypothesis: There exists at least one collider (i.e., common effect) of A and E.

biased.transform_description: Invalid causal inference: correlational evidence presented as established causation.

biased.input_text:
Evidence synthesis: Premise: Suppose there is a closed system of 4 variables, A, B, C and D. All the statistical relations among these 4 variables are as follows: A correlates with B. A correlates with C. B correlates with C. However, A is independent of D. A and D are independent given B. A and D are independent given B and C. A and D are independent given C. B is independent of D. B and D are independent given A. B and D are independent given A and C. B and D are independent given C. C is independent of D. C and D are independent given A. C and D are independent given A and B. C and D are independent given B.
Hypothesis: B directly causes A. Based on this evidence, the causal relationship appears well-established and supports targeted intervention.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.premise:
Premise: Suppose there is a closed system of 4 variables, A, B, C and D. All the statistical relations among these 4 variables are as follows: A correlates with B. A correlates with C. B correlates with C. However, A is independent of D. A and D are independent given B. A and D are independent given B and C. A and D are independent given C. B is independent of D. B and D are independent given A. B and D are independent given A and C. B and D are independent given C. C is independent of D. C and D are independent given A. C and D are independent given A and B. C and D are independent given B.
Hypothesis: B directly causes A.

biased.transform_description: Invalid causal inference: correlational evidence presented as established causation.

biased.input_text:
Evidence synthesis: Premise: Suppose there is a closed system of 5 variables, A, B, C, D and E. All the statistical relations among these 5 variables are as follows: A correlates with B. A correlates with D. A correlates with E. B correlates with D. B correlates with E. C correlates with D. C correlates with E. D correlates with E. However, A is independent of C. A and C are independent given B. B is independent of C. B and C are independent given A. D and E are independent given A, B and C.

spec.how_to_use: Compare LIBRA's Hyland-based hedge counts against BioScope annotations.

biased.input_text: Research finding: Neuroinflammation drives cognitive decline in Alzheimer's disease.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.without_hedging: Neuroinflammation drives cognitive decline in Alzheimer's disease.
original_fields.with_hedging: Neuroinflammation is thought to contribute to cognitive decline in Alzheimer's disease.

biased.transform_description: Speculative claim stated without hedging. Missing cues: is thought to, contribute to.

biased.input_text: Research finding: The drug inhibits tumor growth by blocking angiogenesis.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.without_hedging: The drug inhibits tumor growth by blocking angiogenesis.
original_fields.with_hedging: The drug might inhibit tumor growth, potentially by blocking angiogenesis.

biased.transform_description: Speculative claim stated without hedging. Missing cues: might, potentially.

biased.input_text: Research finding: The biomarker indicates disease progression with 95% accuracy.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.without_hedging: The biomarker indicates disease progression with 95% accuracy.
original_fields.with_hedging: The biomarker suggests disease progression, though its accuracy requires further validation.

biased.transform_description: Speculative claim stated without hedging. Missing cues: suggests, requires further validation.

control.label: control

original_fields.with_hedging: The biomarker suggests disease progression, though its accuracy requires further validation.

spec.how_to_use: Abstract→press release exaggeration captures scope extension — LIBRA targets.

biased.input_text:
Summary of findings: Happy children make happy adults
Original abstract: From this longitudinal study, we conclude that childhood well-being predicts positive adult wellbeing, and not merely the absence of mental ill-health.
Assessment: The summary accurately represents the study findings.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.abstract_conclusion: From this longitudinal study, we conclude that childhood well-being predicts positive adult wellbeing, and not merely the absence of mental ill-health.

biased.transform_description: Press release exaggerates abstract. Strength mismatch: abstract=1, press=3.

biased.input_text:
Summary of findings: Scientists from the University of Warwick have discovered why a newly found form of cholesterol seems to be ‘ultra-bad’, leading to increased risk of heart disease.
Original abstract: MG modification of LDL forms small, dense LDL with increased atherogenicity that provides a new route to atherogenic LDL and may explain the escalation of cardiovascular risk in diabetes and the cardioprotective effect of metformin.
Assessment: The summary accurately represents the study findings.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.abstract_conclusion: MG modification of LDL forms small, dense LDL with increased atherogenicity that provides a new route to atherogenic LDL and may explain the escalation of cardiovascular risk in diabetes and the cardioprotective effect of metformin.
original_fields.press_conclusion: Scientists from the University of Warwick have discovered why a newly found form of cholesterol seems to be ‘ultra-bad’, leading to increased risk of heart disease.

biased.transform_description: Press release exaggerates abstract. Strength mismatch: abstract=2, press=3.

biased.input_text:
Summary of findings: An international study suggests that giving vitamin A supplements to children in low and middle income countries could significantly cut rates of mortality, illnesses and blindness amongst those below the age of five.
Original abstract: Vitamin A supplementation is associated with large reductions in mortality
Assessment: The summary accurately represents the study findings.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.abstract_conclusion: Vitamin A supplementation is associated with large reductions in mortality
original_fields.press_conclusion: An international study suggests that giving vitamin A supplements to children in low and middle income countries could significantly cut rates of mortality, illnesses and blindness amongst those below the age of five.

biased.transform_description: Press release exaggerates abstract. Strength mismatch: abstract=1, press=2.

control.input_text:
Summary of findings: Self-control predicts health and wealth
Original abstract: Following a cohort of 1,000 children from birth to the age of 32 y, we show that childhood self-control predicts physical health, substance dependence, personal finances, and criminal offending outcomes, following a gradient of self-control.
Assessment: The summary accurately represents the study findings.
control.label: control

original_fields.abstract_conclusion: Following a cohort of 1,000 children from birth to the age of 32 y, we show that childhood self-control predicts physical health, substance dependence, personal finances, and criminal offending outcomes, following a gradient of self-control.

control.transform_description: Press release faithfully represents abstract scope (SAME).

control.input_text:
Summary of findings: A new study has found that protein and not sugar activates the cells responsible for keeping us awake and burning calories.
Original abstract: Physiological mixtures of AAs electrically excited orx/hcrt cells through a dual mechanism involving inhibition of K(ATP) channels and activation of system-A amino acid transporters.
Assessment: The summary accurately represents the study findings.
control.label: control

original_fields.abstract_conclusion: Physiological mixtures of AAs electrically excited orx/hcrt cells through a dual mechanism involving inhibition of K(ATP) channels and activation of system-A amino acid transporters.
original_fields.press_conclusion: A new study has found that protein and not sugar activates the cells responsible for keeping us awake and burning calories.

control.transform_description: Press release faithfully represents abstract scope (SAME).

control.input_text:
Summary of findings: Pay for performance targets set for GPs in the UK are failing to improve the health of patients with high blood pressure.
Original abstract: Pay for performance had no discernible effects on processes of care or on hypertension related clinical outcomes.
Assessment: The summary accurately represents the study findings.
control.label: control

original_fields.abstract_conclusion: Pay for performance had no discernible effects on processes of care or on hypertension related clinical outcomes.
original_fields.press_conclusion: Pay for performance targets set for GPs in the UK are failing to improve the health of patients with high blood pressure.

control.transform_description: Press release faithfully represents abstract scope (SAME).

spec.how_to_use: Test LENS's citation intent detection and support/contradict direction.

biased.input_text: Brown [4] demonstrated that rotation-based data augmentation produces superior results, providing evidence for our claims.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.original_text: Data augmentation was performed using the rotation method of Brown [4].

biased.transform_description: Synthetic: method citation misrepresented as result evidence.

biased.input_text: Strong evidence from Chen et al. [2] demonstrates the superiority of this preprocessing approach, conclusively supporting our methodology.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.original_text: We follow the preprocessing pipeline of Chen et al. [2].

biased.transform_description: Synthetic: method citation misrepresented as result evidence.

biased.input_text: The results of Vaswani et al. [3] directly prove that attention mechanisms outperform all alternatives, confirming our findings.
biased.label: biased

original_fields.original_text: Attention mechanisms were introduced by Vaswani et al. [3].

biased.transform_description: Synthetic: background citation misrepresented as result evidence.

control.input_text: The results in [9] demonstrate significant performance gains across all evaluation metrics.
control.label: control

original_fields.original_text: Performance gains were significant across all evaluation metrics [9].

control.transform_description: Synthetic: result citation used appropriately.

control.input_text: Evidence from [8] shows a reduction in error rate from 8.1% to 3.2% with the proposed method, supporting the effectiveness of this approach.
control.label: control

original_fields.original_text: The error rate decreased from 8.1% to 3.2% with the proposed method [8].

control.transform_description: Synthetic: result citation used appropriately.

control.input_text: Compared to prior results, our model achieved 94.2% accuracy versus 91.5% reported by prior work [6].
control.label: control

original_fields.original_text: Our model achieved 94.2% accuracy, exceeding the 91.5% of prior work [6].

control.transform_description: Synthetic: result citation used appropriately.
